ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #14, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Reed for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Reed stated that he had received the Examiner's notes. The Court addressed the jump in time spent on the 11th/12th account to the eleven hours spent on this account. Mr. Reed stated that this was for two accountings for two separate trust accounts. Additional discussions regarding the fees were held. Mr. Reed stated that he had worked with one Examiner on some deficencies, that a second Examiner had raised additional issues with the format, and that he was working toward sorting out the proper accounting format to avoid issues in the future. The Court stated that after the format issue is sorted out then the fees should revert. Mr. Reed stated that once the format is set a paralegal should be able to prepare it and he would review it. The Court stated the time spent on the 11th account should compare to the time spent on the next account. The Court noted it would look at that next year, and granted the petition with that understanding (signing both orders in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.
